factors.name: Fed Policy Hawkish Surprise
factors.summary: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh delivered hawkish tilt in first FOMC decision (per FXStreet 17:24), signaling rates held higher for longer. This directly pressures real yields and compresses gold valuations.

factors.name: US Real Yields Rising
factors.summary: US Treasury yields have risen sharply since Wednesday FOMC (per FXStreet 22:01, 17:46), with hawkish Fed repricing lifting yields and depressing gold. Real yields are the single-most correlated factor to gold.

factors.name: US Dollar Strength (DXY Rally)
factors.summary: DXY rallying on prospect of Fed rate hold and hawkish forward guidance (FXStreet 21:54); USD/JPY at highest weekly close since 1986 (19:27). Stronger USD is directly inverse to gold prices.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk-Off (Iran Deal Settled)
factors.summary: US-Iran peace deal and Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire (announced 19:18-19:05) have dramatically reduced safe-haven demand. Brent crude back to $80, unwinding entire 4-month war premium (FXStreet 22:09). War premium removal removes key gold bid.

factors.name: Price Action Extension
factors.summary: Gold has already fallen -3.89% in 24h and -0.93% in 4h; FXStreet (17:46) notes third consecutive week of losses. Move is extended, late-stage selling phase.

factors.name: Oil Price Collapse
factors.summary: Oil tanker traffic jump and Iran deal implementation causing oil to fall back to pre-war levels (Brent $80, WTI $77 per FXStreet 22:09). Lower oil reduces inflation expectations and cuts safe-haven bid for gold.

factors.name: Competing Safe-Haven: USD Strength
factors.summary: USD Index rally is siphoning flows away from gold; CHF and other traditional havens are also weakening (FXStreet 16:12, 16:37). Dollar is the dominant safe-haven in current regime.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment Pivot
factors.summary: Equities rallied Thursday-Friday post-FOMC despite hawkish surprise (risk-on reset after geopolitical relief). Gold typically underperforms in risk-on regimes when real yields are rising.

screening.narrative: Gold is in a bear phase following a perfect storm of headwinds: the new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh signaled hawkish policy and higher-for-longer rates, real yields have spiked sharply since Wednesday, the USD Index has surged on rate expectations, and the Iran-US peace deal has eliminated the geopolitical premium that supported safe-haven flows. The move is already extended (down -3.89% in 24h), indicating we are in PHASE 3 of a selloff. While the ceasefire news initially lifted gold to $4,382 earlier in the week, the FOMC decision reversed all gains and then some, exposing the fragility of the geopolitical bid once macro hawkishness reasserted itself.
catalysts_ahead: US PCE inflation data (date TBD in coming days per FXStreet 20:43) — cool print would be BULLISH gold, hot print would extend selling
Fed Governor Waller speech (Monday per 18:00 note) — hawkish tone could deepen bearish pressure
Global PMI releases (per FXStreet 20:43) — weakness could eventually support gold but unlikely to offset Fed tightening bias
US equity earnings / tech sector momentum — continued risk-on could pressure gold further if yields stay elevated
screening.entry_guidance: Move is extended and late-stage; avoid chasing shorts here. Wait for retest of $4,200–$4,220 resistance or any intraday bounce before re-shorting. If PCE comes in cool or Fed commentary softens Monday, this bias could flip — watch for $4,250+ resistance break as re-entry signal for longs.
screening.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) real yields roll over and 10y TIPS dips below recent lows; (2) PCE inflation surprise significantly below expectations, causing Fed repricing dovish; (3) ceasefire collapses and geopolitical premium re-ignites (early warning: Lebanon clashes resuming despite ceasefire per 16:04 and 15:47 reports); (4) USD momentum stalls and DXY reverses. Monitor Iran nuclear talks resumption timeline and Israeli-Hezbollah ceasefire stability closely — fragility noted in multiple reports.

factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy
factors.summary: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh delivered a hawkish hold with rates kept 'higher for longer'. FXStreet reports 'Gold answers to the Fed, not the fear' and 'The US Dollar remembers how to rally' on prospect of Fed rate hike, causing gold to extend selloff for third consecutive week.

factors.name: US Dollar Strength
factors.summary: DXY rallying on hawkish Fed repricing. USD/JPY hit 161.81 (highest weekly close since 1986), USD/CAD up 1.34% weekly, EUR/USD fell despite ECB rate hike. Broad-based dollar strength is negative for dollar-denominated gold.

factors.name: Real Yields / Treasury Yields
factors.summary: FXStreet notes 'rising US Treasury yields, which have aimed higher since Wednesday amid the Fed's hawkish tilt'. Higher real yields make non-yielding gold less attractive; this is the single most correlated factor and it's moving against gold.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Premium Unwinding
factors.summary: US-Iran deal reached with Strait of Hormuz reopened; oil down sharply (Brent back near $80 after 'handing back almost the entire premium built over nearly four months of open war'). Safe-haven bid unwinding, though new ceasefire with Hezbollah is 'shaky' with strikes continuing.

factors.name: Price Action / Momentum
factors.summary: Gold down -3.89% in 24h, -1.69% on Friday, selling for third consecutive day and third consecutive week. FXStreet: 'Gold price edges lower...weighed by overall US Dollar strength sparked by the Federal Reserve's decision to keep interest rates higher for longer.'

factors.name: Oil Prices
factors.summary: Brent near $80, WTI near $77 after Iran deal. Lower oil is disinflationary (slightly bullish for gold longer-term) but immediate effect is risk-on/safe-haven unwind (bearish). Mixed signal, slight bearish tilt near-term.

factors.name: Central Bank Policy Divergence
factors.summary: ECB hiked rates for first time since 2023 yet EUR fell (hawkish Fed overwhelming). BoJ remains dovish with USD/JPY at 1986 highs despite intervention threats. Fed's relative hawkishness strengthens dollar against all majors, pressuring gold.

factors.name: Market Positioning
factors.summary: Canadian Dollar 'ditches Crude Oil for Gold' per FXStreet, suggesting commodities complex repositioning. Gold losing safe-haven appeal as FXStreet notes: 'Gold is supposed to be the asset you want when the world looks dangerous, which makes this week's price action quietly remarkable.'

factors.name: Upcoming Data Risk
factors.summary: FXStreet preview notes 'US Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) data' coming next week alongside 'global PMI releases'. PCE is key inflation gauge; could provide directional catalyst but uncertainty caps conviction now.

confirmation.narrative: Gold has suffered a sharp three-day, three-week decline driven by the one-two punch of geopolitical risk premium unwinding (US-Iran deal) and a surprisingly hawkish Fed Chair Warsh prioritizing higher-for-longer rates. The -3.89% 24h drop from $4,333 (implied) to $4,167 reflects markets repricing gold's drivers: higher real yields and a surging dollar now trump war premium. The move is extended in Phase 3, having already traveled ~5% from the $4,382 peak to current levels, with gold now testing support near recent lows around $4,022.
catalysts_ahead: US PCE inflation data (week ahead per FXStreet)
Iran-US nuclear talks resumption (delayed but planned)
Lebanon ceasefire stability (extremely fragile, strikes continuing)
confirmation.risk_warning: Bearish bias invalidated if: (1) Iran deal collapses and war premium returns (ceasefire already breaking down with 'two killed in drone strike' post-ceasefire), (2) Fed pivots dovish or Warsh walks back hawkish tone, (3) PCE comes in significantly cooler than expected prompting rate-cut repricing, or (4) dollar rally exhausts and DXY reverses sharply. Gold has already moved ~5% from peak; chasing shorts here risky without confirmation.
confirmation.entry_guidance: Move is extended Phase 3; avoid chasing current weakness. Wait for a relief bounce toward $4,200-$4,220 resistance zone to consider fresh shorts, or for break and hold below $4,140 with confirmation before adding bearish exposure.

reason: DISAGREEMENT: Haiku=SELL vs Sonnet=BEARISH — silent log

factors.name: Fed Policy - Hawkish Repricing
factors.summary: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh signaled higher-for-longer rates at first FOMC under his tenure (mentioned in MarketWatch 2026-06-19T17:24). Markets repriced in more hawkish rate path; this directly suppresses real yields and crushes gold. This is THE dominant driver this week.

factors.name: Real Yields / US Treasury Yields
factors.summary: Rising US Treasury yields cited as primary headwind for gold (FXStreet 2026-06-19T17:46). Gold edges lower as Fed's hawkish stance lifts yields and real rates, removing rate-cut expectations that would otherwise support safe-haven demand.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: USD rallying strongly on prospect of higher Fed rates (FXStreet 2026-06-19T21:54 'US Dollar remembers how to rally'). DXY strength is inverse to gold; stronger dollar makes gold more expensive for foreign buyers and reduces safe-haven appeal.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran-US Deal)
factors.summary: US-Iran interim peace deal announced (multiple sources, e.g., Al Jazeera 2026-06-19T02:36, investinglive 2026-06-19T19:27). Risk-off premium unwind removes safe-haven bid from gold; oil dropped ~$3 (Brent to $80), reducing inflation hedging narrative for bullion.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations / PCE Upcoming
factors.summary: No fresh CPI/PCE print in news window, but FXStreet (2026-06-19T20:43) flags 'upcoming week' will bring US PCE data and Fed policy test. Key inflation data release likely Monday-Tuesday could be a catalyst; current trend is lower inflation = lower breakevens = bearish gold.

factors.name: BoJ Yen Support / USD/JPY
factors.summary: USD/JPY hit 161.80 (highest weekly close since 1986 per investinglive 2026-06-19T19:27), though BoJ Finance Minister hinted at 'decisive action' intervention. Higher USD/JPY unwinds yen carry-trade safe-haven flows; spillover weakness in commodities and gold.

factors.name: ECB Rate Hike
factors.summary: ECB delivered first rate hike since 2023, yet Euro sank (FXStreet 2026-06-19T21:58 'Euro sinks on its own rate hike'). Divergence suggests broader growth concerns; mixed signal for gold — higher rates bearish, but growth anxiety could provide mild support.

factors.name: Price Action (24h & 4h moves)
factors.summary: Gold down 3.76% in 24h and 0.86% in 4h, indicating PHASE_2/3 selloff underway. Move is extended on recent Fed hawkish repricing; pullback or consolidation likely before fresh downside.

factors.name: Oil Prices & Inflation Hedge
factors.summary: Brent crude near $80, WTI near $77 (FXStreet 2026-06-19T22:09), down ~$3 from war premium after Iran deal. Lower oil = lower inflation expectations = less need for gold inflation hedge; removes structural bid.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying
factors.summary: No fresh news on PBoC, RBI, or other CB gold purchases in news window. Structural CB bid remains, but overwhelmed by short-term Fed repricing and USD strength.

screening.narrative: Gold entered this week on SELL bias (per your 2026-06-20 00:11 read: 'SELL 7/10 → BEARISH 7/10'). The move has accelerated sharply following Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's hawkish first FOMC—signaling rates higher-for-longer—and the US-Iran interim peace deal, which unwound the risk-premium bid that had supported bullion. Real yields are rising and the USD is rallying hard (DXY strength, USD/JPY at 2-year highs). Oil crashed from $83+ to $80, removing the inflation-hedge narrative. The 24h selloff of 3.76% is a Phase 2/3 move—already underway, momentum extended, pullback likely before fresh sell signals.
catalysts_ahead: US PCE inflation data (week of 2026-06-23, likely Tuesday/Wednesday) — cool print = more bearish for gold; hot print = potential relief rally
ISM Manufacturing / Services PMI (early week) — weakness = growth scare = mild gold support; strength = more hawkish Fed = bearish gold
Fed speakers (Warsh, Williams, Waller) — any dovish pivot would be bullish gold; hawkish hold would be bearish
Lebanon/Israel ceasefire stability — further fragility could reignite geopolitical bid; hold suggests risk-off unwind continues
BoJ intervention or fresh yen policy guidance — could flip USD/JPY and ripple through commodities
screening.risk_warning: If PCE or upcoming inflation data comes in HOTTER than expected (e.g., Core PCE >0.4% m/m), real yields may not rise as much as priced, and gold could stabilize/recover. Alternatively, if Lebanon ceasefire collapses or new geopolitical shock erupts (Taiwan, Russia-Ukraine escalation), safe-haven bid could return sharply. A dovish Warsh comment or hint of rate pause would flip this bias bullish immediately.
screening.entry_guidance: Move already extended down 3.76% in 24h; avoid chasing lower. Better entry on pullback to $4,190–$4,210 resistance zone with confirmed rejection (bearish candlestick), or wait for PCE data Tuesday before fresh shorts. If gold bounces >$4,200, reduce position size until a new lower low breaks $4,140 support.
